education_analysis

HaoranLi

2026-03-17

1. Introduction

This study examines:

  1. What parenting styles are reported by urban Chinese parents?
  2. What is the relationship between parenting styles and parent-child relationship quality?

2. Setup

library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(psych)
library(GPArotation)
library(broom)
library(ggplot2)
library(corrplot)
library(lavaan)
library(semPlot)

3.Data import

df <- read_excel("SPSS_data.xlsx")
df <- df %>% rename_with(trimws)

4. Data Preprocessing

4.1 Define Items

ps_items <- paste0("PS-Q", 1:24)
pcr_items <- paste0("PCR-Q", 1:30)

ps_reverse <- paste0("PS-Q", 16:24)
pcr_reverse <- c("PCR-Q2","PCR-Q4","PCR-Q6","PCR-Q7","PCR-Q12","PCR-Q14","PCR-Q17","PCR-Q19","PCR-Q21","PCR-Q23","PCR-Q24","PCR-Q25")

4.2 Reverse Coding

df2 <- df

df2[ps_reverse] <- lapply(df2[ps_reverse], function(x) 6 - x)
df2[pcr_reverse] <- lapply(df2[pcr_reverse], function(x) 6 - x)

4.3 Scale Scores

df2 <- df2 %>%
  mutate(
    PS_score = rowMeans(select(., all_of(ps_items)), na.rm = TRUE),
    PCR_score = rowMeans(select(., all_of(pcr_items)), na.rm = TRUE)
  )

5. PCA Analysis

5.1 Parenting Style

ps_data <- df2 %>% select(all_of(ps_items)) %>% na.omit()

ps_pca <- principal(ps_data, nfactors = 3, rotate = "varimax", scores = TRUE)
ps_pca
## Principal Components Analysis
## Call: principal(r = ps_data, nfactors = 3, rotate = "varimax", scores = TRUE)
## Standardized loadings (pattern matrix) based upon correlation matrix
##         RC1  RC2   RC3   h2   u2 com
## PS-Q1  0.75 0.37  0.14 0.72 0.28 1.5
## PS-Q2  0.80 0.33  0.14 0.76 0.24 1.4
## PS-Q3  0.82 0.32 -0.03 0.77 0.23 1.3
## PS-Q4  0.80 0.32  0.04 0.75 0.25 1.3
## PS-Q5  0.76 0.35  0.16 0.73 0.27 1.5
## PS-Q6  0.75 0.38  0.20 0.75 0.25 1.6
## PS-Q7  0.72 0.32  0.31 0.72 0.28 1.8
## PS-Q8  0.80 0.36 -0.09 0.77 0.23 1.4
## PS-Q9  0.80 0.35 -0.10 0.76 0.24 1.4
## PS-Q10 0.82 0.34 -0.09 0.80 0.20 1.4
## PS-Q11 0.81 0.29 -0.09 0.75 0.25 1.3
## PS-Q12 0.77 0.29  0.09 0.68 0.32 1.3
## PS-Q13 0.80 0.37 -0.04 0.78 0.22 1.4
## PS-Q14 0.81 0.33 -0.06 0.76 0.24 1.3
## PS-Q15 0.79 0.34 -0.07 0.75 0.25 1.4
## PS-Q16 0.32 0.76 -0.31 0.77 0.23 1.7
## PS-Q17 0.26 0.81 -0.09 0.72 0.28 1.2
## PS-Q18 0.29 0.76  0.09 0.67 0.33 1.3
## PS-Q19 0.44 0.71  0.01 0.70 0.30 1.7
## PS-Q20 0.36 0.76  0.15 0.73 0.27 1.5
## PS-Q21 0.29 0.78  0.26 0.76 0.24 1.5
## PS-Q22 0.39 0.74  0.23 0.76 0.24 1.7
## PS-Q23 0.43 0.72 -0.13 0.71 0.29 1.7
## PS-Q24 0.48 0.68 -0.12 0.71 0.29 1.9
## 
##                         RC1  RC2  RC3
## SS loadings           10.53 6.73 0.54
## Proportion Var         0.44 0.28 0.02
## Cumulative Var         0.44 0.72 0.74
## Proportion Explained   0.59 0.38 0.03
## Cumulative Proportion  0.59 0.97 1.00
## 
## Mean item complexity =  1.5
## Test of the hypothesis that 3 components are sufficient.
## 
## The root mean square of the residuals (RMSR) is  0.02 
##  with the empirical chi square  91.26  with prob <  1 
## 
## Fit based upon off diagonal values = 1
print(ps_pca$loadings, cutoff = 0.3)
## 
## Loadings:
##        RC1    RC2    RC3   
## PS-Q1   0.749  0.370       
## PS-Q2   0.798  0.330       
## PS-Q3   0.816  0.322       
## PS-Q4   0.804  0.322       
## PS-Q5   0.762  0.349       
## PS-Q6   0.751  0.379       
## PS-Q7   0.723  0.324  0.305
## PS-Q8   0.797  0.359       
## PS-Q9   0.795  0.350       
## PS-Q10  0.819  0.343       
## PS-Q11  0.813              
## PS-Q12  0.768              
## PS-Q13  0.803  0.372       
## PS-Q14  0.809  0.325       
## PS-Q15  0.794  0.344       
## PS-Q16  0.321  0.756 -0.315
## PS-Q17         0.806       
## PS-Q18         0.756       
## PS-Q19  0.443  0.708       
## PS-Q20  0.364  0.758       
## PS-Q21         0.784       
## PS-Q22  0.390  0.743       
## PS-Q23  0.430  0.716       
## PS-Q24  0.477  0.683       
## 
##                   RC1   RC2   RC3
## SS loadings    10.529 6.733 0.541
## Proportion Var  0.439 0.281 0.023
## Cumulative Var  0.439 0.719 0.742

5.2 Parent-Child Relationship

pcr_data <- df2 %>% select(all_of(pcr_items)) %>% na.omit()

pcr_pca <- principal(pcr_data, nfactors = 3, rotate = "varimax", scores = TRUE)
pcr_pca
## Principal Components Analysis
## Call: principal(r = pcr_data, nfactors = 3, rotate = "varimax", scores = TRUE)
## Standardized loadings (pattern matrix) based upon correlation matrix
##           RC1   RC3   RC2   h2   u2 com
## PCR-Q1   0.70  0.37  0.19 0.67 0.33 1.7
## PCR-Q2   0.39  0.75  0.11 0.73 0.27 1.5
## PCR-Q3   0.67  0.33  0.30 0.65 0.35 1.9
## PCR-Q4   0.37  0.69  0.09 0.62 0.38 1.6
## PCR-Q5   0.76  0.37  0.18 0.75 0.25 1.6
## PCR-Q6   0.18  0.76  0.18 0.64 0.36 1.2
## PCR-Q7   0.37  0.71  0.16 0.67 0.33 1.6
## PCR-Q8   0.78  0.32  0.20 0.75 0.25 1.5
## PCR-Q9   0.37  0.21  0.62 0.58 0.42 1.9
## PCR-Q10  0.77  0.33  0.27 0.77 0.23 1.6
## PCR-Q11  0.66  0.32  0.38 0.69 0.31 2.1
## PCR-Q12  0.26  0.74  0.20 0.66 0.34 1.4
## PCR-Q13  0.46  0.15  0.56 0.54 0.46 2.1
## PCR-Q14  0.31  0.73  0.16 0.65 0.35 1.5
## PCR-Q15  0.31  0.17  0.69 0.60 0.40 1.5
## PCR-Q16  0.74  0.31  0.30 0.73 0.27 1.7
## PCR-Q17  0.34  0.76  0.10 0.70 0.30 1.4
## PCR-Q18  0.24  0.10  0.77 0.65 0.35 1.2
## PCR-Q19 -0.18 -0.10 -0.79 0.67 0.33 1.1
## PCR-Q20  0.51  0.28  0.49 0.58 0.42 2.6
## PCR-Q21 -0.24 -0.13 -0.75 0.64 0.36 1.3
## PCR-Q22  0.55  0.34  0.45 0.62 0.38 2.6
## PCR-Q23 -0.14 -0.13 -0.79 0.66 0.34 1.1
## PCR-Q24  0.20  0.76  0.22 0.67 0.33 1.3
## PCR-Q25  0.31  0.75  0.17 0.68 0.32 1.4
## PCR-Q26  0.67  0.39  0.30 0.68 0.32 2.0
## PCR-Q27  0.08  0.16  0.79 0.66 0.34 1.1
## PCR-Q28  0.51  0.30  0.48 0.59 0.41 2.6
## PCR-Q29  0.69  0.39  0.26 0.70 0.30 1.9
## PCR-Q30  0.67  0.36  0.26 0.64 0.36 1.9
## 
##                        RC1  RC3  RC2
## SS loadings           7.39 6.62 5.85
## Proportion Var        0.25 0.22 0.19
## Cumulative Var        0.25 0.47 0.66
## Proportion Explained  0.37 0.33 0.29
## Cumulative Proportion 0.37 0.71 1.00
## 
## Mean item complexity =  1.7
## Test of the hypothesis that 3 components are sufficient.
## 
## The root mean square of the residuals (RMSR) is  0.02 
##  with the empirical chi square  164.8  with prob <  1 
## 
## Fit based upon off diagonal values = 1
print(pcr_pca$loadings, cutoff = 0.3)
## 
## Loadings:
##         RC1    RC3    RC2   
## PCR-Q1   0.703  0.375       
## PCR-Q2   0.389  0.752       
## PCR-Q3   0.673  0.328  0.303

6. Factor Scores

ps_scores <- as.data.frame(ps_pca$scores)
colnames(ps_scores) <- c("PS_authoritative","PS_authoritarian","PS_permissive")

pcr_scores <- as.data.frame(pcr_pca$scores)
colnames(pcr_scores) <- c("PCR_closeness","PCR_dependency","PCR_conflict")

complete_index <- df2 %>%
  select(all_of(c(ps_items, pcr_items))) %>%
  complete.cases()

analysis_df <- bind_cols(
  df2[complete_index, ],
  ps_scores,
  pcr_scores
)

7. Descriptive Statistics

describe(analysis_df %>%
  select(PS_authoritative, PS_authoritarian, PS_permissive,
         PCR_closeness, PCR_dependency, PCR_conflict))
##                  vars   n mean sd median trimmed  mad   min  max range  skew
## PS_authoritative    1 506    0  1   0.06   -0.02 0.99 -3.94 3.18  7.12  0.08
## PS_authoritarian    2 506    0  1   0.06    0.04 1.08 -3.87 3.74  7.61 -0.54
## PS_permissive       3 506    0  1   0.02    0.02 0.87 -3.60 3.01  6.61 -0.25
## PCR_closeness       4 506    0  1  -0.09   -0.03 1.01 -3.80 2.72  6.51  0.20
## PCR_dependency      5 506    0  1   0.08    0.04 1.07 -3.83 3.64  7.47 -0.63
## PCR_conflict        6 506    0  1  -0.06   -0.01 1.15 -2.70 2.24  4.94  0.08
##                  kurtosis   se
## PS_authoritative     0.52 0.04
## PS_authoritarian     1.23 0.04
## PS_permissive        0.81 0.04
## PCR_closeness        0.08 0.04
## PCR_dependency       1.68 0.04
## PCR_conflict        -0.70 0.04

8. Correlation Analysis

corr_data <- analysis_df %>%
  select(PS_authoritative, PS_authoritarian, PS_permissive,
         PCR_closeness, PCR_dependency, PCR_conflict)

corr_mat <- cor(corr_data, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
corr_mat
##                  PS_authoritative PS_authoritarian PS_permissive PCR_closeness
## PS_authoritative     1.000000e+00    -5.236309e-16 -6.348647e-16  3.095028e-01
## PS_authoritarian    -5.236309e-16     1.000000e+00  6.234643e-16  4.255917e-02
## PS_permissive       -6.348647e-16     6.234643e-16  1.000000e+00 -1.260181e-01
## PCR_closeness        3.095028e-01     4.255917e-02 -1.260181e-01  1.000000e+00
## PCR_dependency      -1.432264e-03    -3.147759e-02 -7.424708e-02  4.561931e-16
## PCR_conflict        -1.533230e-01    -3.926592e-02  5.621754e-02  1.374633e-15
##                  PCR_dependency  PCR_conflict
## PS_authoritative  -1.432264e-03 -1.533230e-01
## PS_authoritarian  -3.147759e-02 -3.926592e-02
## PS_permissive     -7.424708e-02  5.621754e-02
## PCR_closeness      4.561931e-16  1.374633e-15
## PCR_dependency     1.000000e+00 -2.913596e-16
## PCR_conflict      -2.913596e-16  1.000000e+00
corrplot(corr_mat, method = "color",
         addCoef.col = "black",
         tl.col = "black",
         number.cex = 0.7)

9. Regression Analysis

model_closeness <- lm(PCR_closeness ~ PS_authoritative + PS_authoritarian + PS_permissive, data = analysis_df)
model_dependency <- lm(PCR_dependency ~ PS_authoritative + PS_authoritarian + PS_permissive, data = analysis_df)
model_conflict <- lm(PCR_conflict ~ PS_authoritative + PS_authoritarian + PS_permissive, data = analysis_df)

summary(model_closeness)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = PCR_closeness ~ PS_authoritative + PS_authoritarian + 
##     PS_permissive, data = analysis_df)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -4.2328 -0.6494 -0.0464  0.6220  2.6283 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                    Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)       3.350e-16  4.198e-02   0.000  1.00000    
## PS_authoritative  3.095e-01  4.202e-02   7.365 7.32e-13 ***
## PS_authoritarian  4.256e-02  4.202e-02   1.013  0.31167    
## PS_permissive    -1.260e-01  4.202e-02  -2.999  0.00285 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.9444 on 502 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.1135, Adjusted R-squared:  0.1082 
## F-statistic: 21.42 on 3 and 502 DF,  p-value: 4.532e-13
summary(model_dependency)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = PCR_dependency ~ PS_authoritative + PS_authoritarian + 
##     PS_permissive, data = analysis_df)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.8467 -0.7031  0.1006  0.7381  3.6408 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                    Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)  
## (Intercept)       4.087e-17  4.444e-02   0.000   1.0000  
## PS_authoritative -1.432e-03  4.449e-02  -0.032   0.9743  
## PS_authoritarian -3.148e-02  4.449e-02  -0.708   0.4795  
## PS_permissive    -7.425e-02  4.449e-02  -1.669   0.0957 .
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.9997 on 502 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.006506,   Adjusted R-squared:  0.0005683 
## F-statistic: 1.096 on 3 and 502 DF,  p-value: 0.3505
summary(model_conflict)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = PCR_conflict ~ PS_authoritative + PS_authoritarian + 
##     PS_permissive, data = analysis_df)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -2.72268 -0.76489 -0.06849  0.74640  2.51232 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                    Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)       2.452e-16  4.395e-02   0.000 1.000000    
## PS_authoritative -1.533e-01  4.400e-02  -3.485 0.000536 ***
## PS_authoritarian -3.927e-02  4.400e-02  -0.892 0.372582    
## PS_permissive     5.622e-02  4.400e-02   1.278 0.201937    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.9887 on 502 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.02821,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.0224 
## F-statistic: 4.858 on 3 and 502 DF,  p-value: 0.002432

10. Standardized Regression

analysis_df_std <- analysis_df %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), scale))

model_closeness_std <- lm(PCR_closeness ~ PS_authoritative + PS_authoritarian + PS_permissive, data = analysis_df_std)
model_dependency_std <- lm(PCR_dependency ~ PS_authoritative + PS_authoritarian + PS_permissive, data = analysis_df_std)
model_conflict_std <- lm(PCR_conflict ~ PS_authoritative + PS_authoritarian + PS_permissive, data = analysis_df_std)

summary(model_closeness_std)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = PCR_closeness ~ PS_authoritative + PS_authoritarian + 
##     PS_permissive, data = analysis_df_std)

## Residuals:

## (Intercept)       6.195e-17  4.198e-02   0.000  1.00000    
## PS_authoritative  3.095e-01  4.202e-02   7.365 7.32e-13 ***
## PS_authoritarian  4.256e-02  4.202e-02   1.013  0.31167    
## PS_permissive    -1.260e-01  4.202e-02  -2.999  0.00285 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.9444 on 502 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.1135, Adjusted R-squared:  0.1082 
## F-statistic: 21.42 on 3 and 502 DF,  p-value: 4.532e-13
summary(model_dependency_std)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = PCR_dependency ~ PS_authoritative + PS_authoritarian + 
##     PS_permissive, data = analysis_df_std)

## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max

## 
## Coefficients:
##                    Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)  
## (Intercept)      -1.270e-18  4.444e-02   0.000   1.0000

## PS_authoritarian -3.148e-02  4.449e-02  -0.708   0.4795  
## PS_permissive    -7.425e-02  4.449e-02  -1.669   0.0957 .
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.9997 on 502 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.006506,   Adjusted R-squared:  0.0005683 
## F-statistic: 1.096 on 3 and 502 DF,  p-value: 0.3505
summary(model_conflict_std)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = PCR_conflict ~ PS_authoritative + PS_authoritarian + 
##     PS_permissive, data = analysis_df_std)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -2.72268 -0.76489 -0.06849  0.74640  2.51232 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                    Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)       2.796e-16  4.395e-02   0.000 1.000000    
## PS_authoritative -1.533e-01  4.400e-02  -3.485 0.000536 ***
## PS_authoritarian -3.927e-02  4.400e-02  -0.892 0.372582    
## PS_permissive     5.622e-02  4.400e-02   1.278 0.201937

## F-statistic: 4.858 on 3 and 502 DF,  p-value: 0.002432

11. Mediation Analysis

med_model <- '
PCR_dependency ~ a*PS_authoritative
PCR_closeness ~ cprime*PS_authoritative + b*PCR_dependency
indirect := a*b
direct := cprime
total := cprime + (a*b)
'

fit_med <- sem(med_model, data = analysis_df, se = "bootstrap", bootstrap = 2000)
summary(fit_med, standardized = TRUE, fit.measures = TRUE)
## lavaan 0.6-21 ended normally after 6 iterations
## 
##   Estimator                                         ML
##   Optimization method                           NLMINB
##   Number of model parameters                         5
## 
##   Number of observations                           506
## 
## Model Test User Model:
##                                                       
##   Test statistic                                 0.000
##   Degrees of freedom                                 0
## 
## Model Test Baseline Model:
## 
##   Test statistic                                50.953
##   Degrees of freedom                                 3
##   P-value                                        0.000
## 
## User Model versus Baseline Model:
## 
##   Comparative Fit Index (CFI)                    1.000
##   Tucker-Lewis Index (TLI)                       1.000
## 
## Loglikelihood and Information Criteria:
## 
##   Loglikelihood user model (H0)              -1409.488
##   Loglikelihood unrestricted model (H1)      -1409.488
##                                                       
##   Akaike (AIC)                                2828.976
##   Bayesian (BIC)                              2850.109
##   Sample-size adjusted Bayesian (SABIC)       2834.238
## 
## Root Mean Square Error of Approximation:
## 
##   RMSEA                                          0.000
##   90 Percent confidence interval - lower         0.000
##   90 Percent confidence interval - upper         0.000
##   P-value H_0: RMSEA <= 0.050                       NA
##   P-value H_0: RMSEA >= 0.080                       NA
## 
## Standardized Root Mean Square Residual:
## 
##   SRMR                                           0.000
## 
## Parameter Estimates:
## 
##   Standard errors                            Bootstrap
##   Number of requested bootstrap draws             2000
##   Number of successful bootstrap draws            2000
## 
## Regressions:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##   PCR_dependency ~                                                      
##     PS_thrt    (a)   -0.001    0.048   -0.030    0.976   -0.001   -0.001
##   PCR_closeness ~                                                       
##     PS_thrt (cprm)    0.310    0.042    7.356    0.000    0.310    0.310
##     PCR_dpn    (b)    0.000    0.073    0.006    0.995    0.000    0.000
## 
## Variances:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##    .PCR_dependency    0.998    0.087   11.476    0.000    0.998    1.000
##    .PCR_closeness     0.902    0.064   14.001    0.000    0.902    0.904
## 
## Defined Parameters:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##     indirect         -0.000    0.004   -0.000    1.000   -0.000   -0.000
##     direct            0.310    0.042    7.356    0.000    0.310    0.310
##     total             0.310    0.042    7.362    0.000    0.310    0.310

12. Path Model

path_model <- '
PCR_closeness ~ PS_authoritative + PS_authoritarian + PS_permissive
PCR_dependency ~ PS_authoritative + PS_authoritarian + PS_permissive
PCR_conflict ~ PS_authoritative + PS_authoritarian + PS_permissive
'

fit_path <- sem(path_model, data = analysis_df)
summary(fit_path, standardized = TRUE)
## lavaan 0.6-21 ended normally after 8 iterations
## 
##   Estimator                                         ML
##   Optimization method                           NLMINB
##   Number of model parameters                        15
## 
##   Number of observations                           506
## 
## Model Test User Model:
##                                                       
##   Test statistic                                 0.000
##   Degrees of freedom                                 0
## 
## Parameter Estimates:
## 
##   Standard errors                             Standard
##   Information                                 Expected
##   Information saturated (h1) model          Structured
## 
## Regressions:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##   PCR_closeness ~                                                       
##     PS_authoritatv    0.310    0.042    7.394    0.000    0.310    0.310
##     PS_authoritarn    0.043    0.042    1.017    0.309    0.043    0.043
##     PS_permissive    -0.126    0.042   -3.011    0.003   -0.126   -0.126
##   PCR_dependency ~                                                      
##     PS_authoritatv   -0.001    0.044   -0.032    0.974   -0.001   -0.001
##     PS_authoritarn   -0.031    0.044   -0.710    0.477   -0.031   -0.031
##     PS_permissive    -0.074    0.044   -1.676    0.094   -0.074   -0.074
##   PCR_conflict ~                                                        
##     PS_authoritatv   -0.153    0.044   -3.499    0.000   -0.153   -0.153
##     PS_authoritarn   -0.039    0.044   -0.896    0.370   -0.039   -0.039
##     PS_permissive     0.056    0.044    1.283    0.200    0.056    0.056
## 
## Covariances:
##                     Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##  .PCR_closeness ~~                                                       
##    .PCR_dependency    -0.008    0.042   -0.182    0.856   -0.008   -0.008
##    .PCR_conflict       0.056    0.041    1.360    0.174    0.056    0.061
##  .PCR_dependency ~~                                                      
##    .PCR_conflict       0.003    0.044    0.062    0.950    0.003    0.003
## 
## Variances:
##                    Estimate  Std.Err  z-value  P(>|z|)   Std.lv  Std.all
##    .PCR_closeness     0.885    0.056   15.906    0.000    0.885    0.887
##    .PCR_dependency    0.992    0.062   15.906    0.000    0.992    0.993
##    .PCR_conflict      0.970    0.061   15.906    0.000    0.970    0.972
semPaths(
  fit_path,
  what = "std",
  layout = "tree",
  edge.label.cex = 1,
  residuals = FALSE,
  intercepts = FALSE
)

13. Conclusion

•   Parenting styles are significantly associated with parent-child relationship quality.
•   Authoritative parenting is positively related to closeness.
•   Authoritarian parenting is associated with conflict.
•   Permissive parenting shows mixed effects.

14. Limitations

•   Cross-sectional data
•   No causal inference
•   Possible unobserved confounders

Appendix

Random forest

library(randomForest)
## randomForest 4.7-1.2
## Type rfNews() to see new features/changes/bug fixes.
## 
## Attaching package: 'randomForest'
## The following object is masked from 'package:ggplot2':
## 
##     margin
## The following object is masked from 'package:psych':
## 
##     outlier
## The following object is masked from 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     combine
rf_model <- randomForest(
  PCR_closeness ~ PS_authoritative + PS_authoritarian + PS_permissive,
  data = analysis_df,
  importance = TRUE
)

rf_model
## 
## Call:
##  randomForest(formula = PCR_closeness ~ PS_authoritative + PS_authoritarian +      PS_permissive, data = analysis_df, importance = TRUE) 
##                Type of random forest: regression
##                      Number of trees: 500
## No. of variables tried at each split: 1
## 
##           Mean of squared residuals: 0.9382516
##                     % Var explained: 5.99
importance(rf_model)
##                    %IncMSE IncNodePurity
## PS_authoritative 37.589814      180.2010
## PS_authoritarian 11.636653      130.1662
## PS_permissive     7.583747      133.8030
varImpPlot(rf_model)

## XGboost

library(xgboost)
## 
## Attaching package: 'xgboost'
## The following object is masked from 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     slice
X <- as.matrix(analysis_df[, c("PS_authoritative","PS_authoritarian","PS_permissive")])
y <- analysis_df$PCR_closeness

dtrain <- xgb.DMatrix(data = X, label = y)

xgb_model <- xgboost(
  data = dtrain,
  nrounds = 100,
  objective = "reg:squarederror",
  verbose = 0
)

importance_matrix <- xgb.importance(
  feature_names = colnames(X),
  model = xgb_model
)

print(importance_matrix)
##             Feature      Gain     Cover Frequency
##              <char>     <num>     <num>     <num>
## 1: PS_authoritative 0.4506000 0.3650953 0.4343875
## 2:    PS_permissive 0.2842934 0.3710142 0.3046165
## 3: PS_authoritarian 0.2651066 0.2638905 0.2609960
xgb.plot.importance(importance_matrix)

## Lasso

library(glmnet)
## Loading required package: Matrix
## Loaded glmnet 4.1-10
X <- as.matrix(analysis_df[, c("PS_authoritative","PS_authoritarian","PS_permissive")])
y <- analysis_df$PCR_closeness

lasso_model <- cv.glmnet(X, y, alpha = 1)

plot(lasso_model)

coef(lasso_model, s = "lambda.min")
## 4 x 1 sparse Matrix of class "dgCMatrix"
##                     lambda.min
## (Intercept)      -1.190807e-16
## PS_authoritative  3.082240e-01
## PS_authoritarian  4.128030e-02
## PS_permissive    -1.247393e-01

Classification

analysis_df <- analysis_df %>%
  mutate(
    closeness_high = ifelse(PCR_closeness > median(PCR_closeness), 1, 0)
  )

rf_class <- randomForest(
  as.factor(closeness_high) ~ PS_authoritative + PS_authoritarian + PS_permissive,
  data = analysis_df
)

rf_class
## 
## Call:
##  randomForest(formula = as.factor(closeness_high) ~ PS_authoritative +      PS_authoritarian + PS_permissive, data = analysis_df) 
##                Type of random forest: classification
##                      Number of trees: 500
## No. of variables tried at each split: 1
## 
##         OOB estimate of  error rate: 44.86%
## Confusion matrix:
##     0   1 class.error
## 0 150 103   0.4071146
## 1 124 129   0.4901186

Explaination

library(iml)

predictor <- Predictor$new(
  rf_model,
  data = analysis_df[, c("PS_authoritative","PS_authoritarian","PS_permissive")],
  y = analysis_df$PCR_closeness
)

imp <- FeatureImp$new(predictor, loss = "mse")
plot(imp)
